stemHtml: What are the possible causes of a hot start, and what are the indications of one? A hot start can be caused by...

options.a: an early starter cut-out, fuel mis-scheduling, or strong tailwinds. The engine may spend a considerable time at very low RPM (sub-idle) but should continue to accelerate, the turbine temperatures may appear high or exceed the limitations.
options.b: a late starter cut-out, insufficient fuel supply, or strong headwinds. The engine will accelerate to flight idle RPM and the turbine temperatures may appear low.
options.c: an early starter cut-out, fuel mis-scheduling, or strong tailwinds. The engine may spend a considerable time at very low RPM (sub-idle) and stops accelerating, the turbine temperatures may appear high or exceed the limitations.
options.d: high outside air temperature, fuel mis-scheduling, or high starter motor RPM. The engine may spend a considerable time at very high RPM but stops accelerating, the turbine temperatures may appear high or exceed the limitations.
